task_completion.description: Google Earth Pro application is launched and visible
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Google Earth Pro application window is open and active on the desktop.
Grid lines (usually yellow or white lines forming a mesh) are visible overlaid on the map/globe.

task_completion.description: Map view is centered on the target coordinates (25°N, 71°W)
task_completion.visual_evidence: The status bar at the bottom of the Google Earth window displays coordinates approximately 25°00'N, 71°00'W, or the visible grid lines are labeled with 25°N and 71°W.

task_completion.description: Zoom level is appropriate to show distinct grid lines over the ocean
task_completion.visual_evidence: The view is zoomed in enough that grid squares are clearly distinguishable, and the background is predominantly blue (ocean).

task_completion.description: Screenshot saved to the correct path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save File' dialog showing the filename 'bermuda_grid.png' being saved to 'Pictures', or a file manager window showing 'bermuda_grid.png' inside the 'Pictures' directory.

integrity.description: Agent used Google Earth Pro GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Google Earth Pro interface (menus, toolbar, search sidebar) rather than a web browser (Google Maps) or command line.

integrity.description: Agent did not fabricate the image in an editor
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots do not show the agent pasting a map image into an image editor (like Paint or GIMP) to add grid lines manually.

integrity.description: Agent navigated using the application tools
integrity.visual_evidence: Evidence of typing coordinates into the search bar or manually dragging/zooming the globe.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target coordinates: 25.0°N, 71.0°W. Region: Atlantic Ocean (Bermuda Triangle). Application: Google Earth Pro. Expected filename: ~/Pictures/bermuda_grid.png.
